headlines.text: Iran FM Araghchi departs Pakistan after delivering Tehran war-end demands to mediators; Witkoff and Kushner still en route
S&P 500 and Nasdaq close at record highs Friday on Intel surge and AI euphoria
US naval blockade redirects 33+ ships near Strait of Hormuz as oil tops $100 a barrel
Michael Jackson biopic 'Michael' stomps to record $85M+ opening weekend despite 39% Rotten Tomatoes score
Trump extends Israel-Lebanon ceasefire by three weeks; IDF still striking southern Lebanon villages
Fed Chair Jerome Powell ousted; Kevin Warsh Senate confirmation hearing underway
National gas average holds near $4.02/gal — highest since August 2022 — with California at $5.84
Helena Bonham Carter abruptly dropped from White Lotus Season 4 just days into France filming
Iran internet blackout hits 57 days straight, over 1,340 hours of severed global access
Zelensky meets Saudi Crown Prince MBS in Riyadh, pitches Ukraine drone expertise as Gulf export

breaking.text: Iran FM leaves Pakistan without direct US talks; Witkoff and Kushner still flying to Islamabad

stories.hed: Witkoff and Kushner fly to Pakistan; Iran says nobody told them
stories.dek: The White House confirmed envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are heading to Islamabad for direct Iran talks — a plan Iran's foreign ministry publicly denied even exists. Araghchi has already left the building, which is a bold diplomatic move even by Schrodinger's-negotiation standards.

stories.hed: US naval blockade on Iran strangles oil routes as Brent holds above $100
stories.dek: The US has redirected at least 33 ships since imposing its naval blockade on April 13, while Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure continues to rattle global energy markets. Iran's parliament speaker says reopening Hormuz is 'not possible' while the blockade remains.

stories.hed: Powell ousted, Warsh confirmation hearing kicks off as Fed rate dilemma sharpens
stories.dek: The Senate Banking Committee began confirmation hearings for Kevin Warsh as Trump's Fed Chair pick, with markets watching closely. Analysts note Warsh is a hawk entering a rate environment complicated by war-driven inflation running at 3.3% annually.

stories.hed: Speaker Johnson unveils FISA 702 extension with April 30 deadline looming
stories.dek: House Speaker Mike Johnson dropped his latest proposal to extend the controversial FISA 702 surveillance program with just days to spare before it expires. Civil liberties groups are not thrilled, which is how you know Congress is probably going to pass it.

stories.hed: Markets at all-time highs while consumer sentiment plunges to all-time lows — pick a lane, America
stories.dek: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq are printing record closes while consumer confidence has cratered to historic lows driven by Iran war anxiety and gas prices up 30% year-over-year. Bank of America's economists warn investors may be dangerously extrapolating the 'escalate to de-escalate' trade war playbook onto an actual war.

markets.context: Intel's blockbuster earnings sent chips and mega-cap AI stocks to new records Friday, though the Dow lagged as cyclicals slid and oil eased slightly on fragile Iran diplomacy hopes.

gas.note: Filling up now costs 35% more than before the Iran war — congrats to everyone who bought an EV and now pays $4.02 in smug savings.

popCulture.hed: Michael Jackson biopic 'Michael' moonwalks to record $85M+ opening despite critics panning it at 39% on Rotten Tomatoes
popCulture.dek: Directed by Antoine Fuqua and starring MJ's real nephew Jaafar Jackson, the film skips the abuse allegations entirely and audiences apparently do not care — giving it an A- CinemaScore. Critics said 'sanitized'; fans said 'shut up and play Billie Jean.'

genZ.definition: An AI-generated TikTok series depicting anthropomorphic fruit characters competing on a Love Island-style dating show, which went so viral it spawned its own 'finale' episode with millions of views. It perfectly captures the April 2026 internet mood of watching chaotic AI slop unironically for emotional support during a wartime news cycle.
genZ.usage: Watching Witkoff fly to Pakistan only to find out Araghchi already left is giving major Fruit Love Island energy — everyone showed up, nobody coupled up, and Pakistan is stuck hosting the reunion special.

tldr: Iran's foreign minister delivered war-end demands to Pakistani mediators then left Islamabad just as US envoys Witkoff and Kushner flew in, turning peace talks into an extremely expensive game of diplomatic tag. Meanwhile Wall Street partied at record highs on Intel earnings while the rest of America paid $4 a gallon and watched a Michael Jackson biopic that pretends the 1990s never happened.
